history.text: In 1639 Lion Gardiner purchased land, what became known as Gardiner's Island, from the Montaukett people.

history.label: 1800s

During the War of 1812, the Gardiners used slaves to transport supplies back and forth to Gardiner's Island.

history.label: 1960s
history.text: The camp was designated as an Air Force Base supporting a 135-foot (41 m)-wide radar (AN/FPS-35) in the early 1960s to detect potential bombers headed for New York City.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Due to storms on Fort Pond Bay, the hamlet of Montauk was actually moved by the Navy at the end of World War II.
